subject: [PATCH v5 07/22] coco/tdx-host: Implement firmware upload sysfs ABI for TDX module updates

reviews.author: Chao Gao
reviews.summary:
Reviewer questioned the necessity of registering seamldr_group, suggesting it be moved to before the check

Reviewer noted that seamldr_group is registered unnecessarily when updates are not supported and suggested moving its registration to before the check.

Reviewer noted that seamldr_group visibility is important and must be hidden on CPUs with an erratum, suggesting moving the registration up before a check to ensure this condition is met
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks a lot for your reviews.

<snip>

---

seamldr_group currently serves only module updates, so registering it when
updates aren't supported seems unnecessary.

---

If new use cases emerge and need seamldr version etc, we can do the
changes.

FWIW, seamldr_group visibility does matter in one case: it must be
hidden on CPUs with an erratum (see
https://lore.kernel.org/kvm/20260315135920.354657-19-chao.gao@intel.com/)

2026-03-18.patch_summary: This patch corrects an issue in the x86/virt/seamldr module where it incorrectly handles memory alignment for a signature (@sig) that is not 4k aligned. The fix involves adjusting the pointer to the next 4k boundary when handling such cases. Additionally, the patch adds a comment to clarify how block->offset_of_module is calculated relative to the start of the struct tdx_blob.
